title: LLM Wiki
summary: LLM-maintained wiki compounds knowledge; RAG re-derives it every query Three-layer architecture: raw sources, wiki, schema

site.title: LLM Wiki
site.description: A pattern for building personal knowledge bases using LLMs
site.author: Andrej Karpathy

absence_patterns: No benchmarks or quantitative comparisons (RAG vs wiki approach)
No discussion of context window limits or scaling beyond ~hundreds of pages
No mention of multi-user collaboration patterns
No discussion of version control conflicts when LLM + human both edit
No treatment of structured data (databases, APIs) as sources
No security/privacy considerations for sensitive knowledge bases
key_claims: LLM-maintained wiki compounds knowledge; RAG re-derives it every query
Three-layer architecture: raw sources, wiki, schema
Index.md + log.md sufficient at ~100 sources / hundreds of pages
Humans abandon wikis because maintenance burden grows faster than value
LLMs solve the Memex maintenance problem Vannevar Bush couldn't
Schema (CLAUDE.md) is the key configuration that makes the LLM disciplined
Good answers should be filed back into wiki as new pages
